contestName: ICPC 武汉全国邀请赛暨湖北省赛

blocks.text: Yachiyo has an integer sequence $S$ of length $n$ and a positive weight $a_i$ for each position. She chooses $m\ge1$ and concatenates $m$ copies of both sequences, obtaining $S'$ and $a'$ of length $mn$. For every $0\le c<m$ and $1\le i\le n$, $S'_{cn+i}=S_i$ and $a'_{cn+i}=a_i$.
She may perform the following operation any number of times:

items: Compact the remaining elements while preserving their order.

blocks.text: Minimize the sum of the remaining weights. Among all ways attaining that minimum, also minimize $m$.
The input has three lines: $n$ ($1 \le n \le 3\times10^5$); $S_1,S_2,\ldots,S_n$ ($1 \le S_i \le 3\times10^5$); and $a_1,a_2,\ldots,a_n$ ($1 \le a_i \le 3\times10^5$).
Output one line containing two integers separated by a space:

items: The first integer is the minimum possible sum of weights of the remaining sequence after the operations.
The second integer is the minimum total number of copies of the original sequence needed to achieve that minimum weight sum.

blocks.text: In this sample, the optimal strategy is to use only 1 copy of the original sequence (that is, m = 1, with no extra copying). The initial sequence S ′ is [1, 1, 4, 5, 1, 4], and the corresponding weight sequence a′ is [1, 9, 1, 9, 8, 10]. Yachiyo can perform the following two operations:

1. Choose i = 1, j = 2 (at this time S1′ = S2′ = 1), and delete the (i + 1)-th through j-th elements (that is, delete the 2nd element). After the operation, S ′ becomes [1, 4, 5, 1, 4], and a′ becomes [1, 1, 9, 8, 10].

2. In the new sequence, choose i = 2, j = 5 (at this time S2′ = S5′ = 4), and delete the (i+1)-th through j-th elements (that is, delete the 3rd, 4th, and 5th elements). After the operation, the remaining S ′ is [1, 4], and the remaining a′ is [1, 1].

At this point, no further elimination is possible, and the sum of the remaining weights is 1 + 1 = 2. It can be proved that under any number of copies and any sequence of operations, the weight sum cannot be smaller than 2.
八千代有一个长度为 $n$ 的整数序列 $S$，每个位置 $i$ 都有正权值 $a_i$。她选择 $m\ge1$，将序列及其权值复制并首尾拼接 $m$ 份，得到长度为 $mn$ 的 $S'$ 与 $a'$。对任意 $0\le c<m$、$1\le i\le n$，都有 $S'_{cn+i}=S_i$ 且 $a'_{cn+i}=a_i$。
她可以执行任意次以下消除操作：

items: 选择满足 $1\le i<j\le |S'|$ 且 $S'_i=S'_j$ 的两个下标 $i,j$。
从 $S'$ 与 $a'$ 中删除第 $i+1$ 到第 $j$ 个元素。
保留剩余元素的相对顺序并重新紧凑排列。

blocks.text: 求剩余序列权值和的最小值；在达到最小权值和的方案中，再求最小的拼接份数 $m$。
输入共三行：第一行是 $n$（$1 \le n \le 3\times10^5$）；第二行是 $S_1,S_2,\ldots,S_n$（$1 \le S_i \le 3\times10^5$）；第三行是 $a_1,a_2,\ldots,a_n$（$1 \le a_i \le 3\times10^5$）。
输出一行包含两个整数，用空格隔开：

items: 第一个整数表示经过操作后，剩余序列的最小权值和。
第二个整数表示为了达到该最小权值和，最少需要的原序列总份数。

blocks.text: 在本样例中，最优策略是仅使用1份原序列（即m=1，不进行额外复制）。初始序列S′为[1,1,4,5,1,4]，对应的权值a′为[1,9,1,9,8,10]。八千代可以进行以下两次操作：
1.选择i=1,j=2（此时S1′=S2′=1），删去第i+1∼j个元素（即删去第2个元素）。操作后序列S′变为[1,4,5,1,4]，权值a′变为[1,1,9,8,10]。
2.在新序列中选择i=2,j=5（此时S2′=S5′=4），删去第i+1∼j个元素（即删去第3,4,5个元素）。操作后序列S′剩余[1,4]，权值a′剩余[1,1]。
此时无法继续消除，剩余序列的权值和为1+1=2。可以证明在任何拼接份数和操作下，权值和不可能小于2。
